question.content: Using symmetry reduction of the triangular resistor network, compute the equivalent resistance between terminals A and B. Output exactly one expression token sequence in plain ASCII only, no Chinese characters, no unit text, no sentence, no equality sign, no chained equalities, and no extra text. Answer format: one final result only. If the result naturally simplifies to a single value, give that value; otherwise give a single algebraic expression using the symbols defined in the problem. Do not include units, explanatory text, or an equals sign.

step_1.content: Use symmetry of the triangle network and remove the four nodes on the perpendicular bisector of AB.
step_1.key_point: Apply symmetry to merge equivalent branches before reduction.

step_3.content: Apply the recurrence R(i+1)=4R[R+R(i)]/(4R+3R(i)) from i=1 to 5 with R(1)=4R/3.
